items.title: Dockers stun the Swans in last quarter blitz to own the top of the ladder

children.content: Fremantle overcomes a rare goalless first half and a 25-point deficit to rout Sydney by 38-points in a pulsating top-of-the-table blockbuster at Perth Stadium.

items.title: Mark McGowan's right-hand man parachuted into plum cabinet role

image.altText: Daniel Pastorelli looks into the distance while standing outside a building.

children.content: Landsdale MP Daniel Pastorelli, the former chief of staff to two premiers, is set to become WA's newest minister after a meteoric rise through Labor's ranks and the resignation of Paul Papalia.

items.title: AC/DC fans celebrate what would have been Bon Scott's 80th birthday

children.content: AC/DC fans gather in Fremantle to celebrate what would have been the 80th birthday of Bon Scott, who passed away in 1980.

children.content: Kylian Mbappé scores yet again to give France a lead over dark horse Morocco in the first of the World Cup quarterfinals. Follow live.

items.title: Mbappé screamer gives France lead over Morocco in World Cup quarterfinal
Hanson says Taylor should stop 'denigrating' One Nation

children.content: Pauline Hanson says Angus Taylor needs to stop "denigrating" One Nation, after the opposition leader delivered his strongest rebuke so far of the right-wing party. Follow live.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson gestures as she speaks in a car seat.

items.title: AI, gambling and Middle East set for Labor conference debate

children.content: A draft document, seen by ABC, reveals AI, gambling and the Middle East are expected to feature heavily in Labor's upcoming national conference debate.

image.altText: A man speaks at a lectern.

items.title: 'We'll sell his body parts': AI extortionists target missing man's parents

children.content: Scammers have sent the parents of a missing West Australian man an AI-generated image of their son and demanded thousands of dollars in exchange for his safety.

items.title: Girls married off as a climate change 'coping strategy'

children.content: Climate change is now believed to be a leading contributor to more frequent and younger nuptials, with 12 million girls tipped to become child brides this year.

image.altText: A young woman holds up her hands covered in henna to the camera.

items.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of power ballad Total Eclipse of the Heart, dies at 75

children.content: Welsh pop singer Bonnie Tyler, ​famed for 1980s power ballads such as Total Eclipse of the ​Heart and Holding Out ​for a Hero, has died.

items.title: Beyoncé-approved: How Hiatus Kayote earned 15 years of A-list respect

children.content: They've made admirers out of music's biggest names over their 15-year career, but this Australian group are always thinking about the future.

items.title: Perth house rents now the third most expensive in the country

children.content: Rents are soaring in several capital cities as low vacancy rates collide with rising interest rates and population growth.

image.altText: A for lease sign at a picket fence

items.title: Mining company hit with record fine for neglecting vulnerable bat in WA

children.content: A Western Australian gold miner is fined more than $100,000 for failing to protect a tiny, orange-furred species of bat in the state's Pilbara region.

items.title: When politics becomes content the risks multiply

children.content: Every press conference, reel and podcast is now a chance for politicians to connect with voters. Yet the strategy that broadens an MP's reach can also amplify their biggest mistakes.

items.title: Police call for help to handle 'disgusting' Facebook comments

image.altText: A medium close-up shot of WA Police Commissioner Col Blanch in uniform.

items.title: McKeown 'gutted' as illness forces Commonwealth Games withdrawal

children.content: Kaylee McKeown withdraws from the Commonwealth Games after glandular fever causes shortness of breath and a loss of appetite.

items.title: Millions of tiny engineers help to restore health of marine ecosystem in WA
